senses.definition: an outburst of anger or frustration, often characterized by uncontrollable emotional and irrational behavior

examples.x: When I told my little sister she couldn't have a second scoop of ice cream, she threw a hissy fit complete with tears and stomping feet.
The customer became increasingly demanding and threw a hissy fit when she was informed that the item she wanted was out of stock.
The actor stormed off the set in a hissy fit after a minor wardrobe malfunction, causing a temporary delay in filming.
My boss had a hissy fit when she discovered a typo in the report, even though it was a minor error easily fixable.
The toddler threw a hissy fit in the grocery store aisle, screaming and throwing items from the shelves, much to the embarrassment of the parents.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone who is extremely furious and is ready to start a fight or argument
said of a woman who is behaving very angrily and cannot be reasoned with, due to being in her menstruation period
used to emphasize or demonstrate that one is extremely unhappy, angry, or irritated

examples.x: Upon hearing the false accusations, she stormed out of the room in high dudgeon, determined to set the record straight.
The politician's controversial remarks left the audience in high dudgeon, with heated protests and demands for an apology.
After his work was plagiarized, the writer responded in high dudgeon, publicly calling out the offender and seeking justice.
The employee resigned from the company in high dudgeon after enduring months of mistreatment and unfair practices.
The artist, upon discovering her artwork had been stolen, expressed her outrage in high dudgeon, vowing to take legal action against the thief.

senses.definition: in a highly agitated or furious state, characterized by the inability to think rationally or make logical decisions

examples.x: After being criticized for her performance, she left the meeting in a huff, slamming the door behind her.
Seeing her ex with another person, she stormed out of the party in a huff, feeling hurt and betrayed.
After losing the game, the athlete threw his equipment on the ground and left the field in a huff.
When the conversation turned to politics, he rolled his eyes and left the room in a huff, unwilling to engage in the discussion.
When she discovered her car had been towed, she was hopping mad and unleashed a string of expletives at the parking attendant.
After waiting for hours at the customer service counter, the irate customer was hopping mad and demanded to speak to the manager.
The team captain lost his temper at the referee's unfair call and went hopping mad, throwing his helmet on the ground in frustration.
Upon receiving the outrageous bill, the restaurant patron became hopping mad and refused to pay, demanding an explanation for the exorbitant charges.
The politician's controversial statement left constituents hopping mad, with protesters gathering outside his office to express their anger and disappointment.

senses.definition: irritated or enraged to the point of frustration

examples.x: He got bent out of shape when his favorite coffee shop ran out of his preferred blend, making a big fuss about it.
She was really bent out of shape when her colleague received recognition for a project she felt she had contributed more to.
The customer became bent out of shape over a minor pricing discrepancy, demanding to speak to the manager and causing a scene.
The politician got bent out of shape over a satirical cartoon, taking personal offense and demanding an apology from the artist.
When the plans for the outing changed slightly, he got bent out of shape and refused to participate, sulking for the rest of the day.

word_list.pronunciation: biː ˌʌp ɪn ˈɑːɹmz ɐbˌaʊt ɔːɹ ˌoʊvɚɹ ɔːɹ ˈæftɚɹ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to be extremely angry or upset about something

examples.x: The community was up in arms about the proposed tax increase.
She was up in arms over the unfair treatment she received.
Parents are up in arms about the new school regulations.
Fans were up in arms over the cancellation of the popular TV show.
The employees were up in arms after the sudden changes in the company policy.

senses.definition: to make someone extremely annoyed or enraged

examples.x: The piercing sound of the alarm clock in the morning sets my teeth on edge.
The sour taste of the spoiled milk made her cringe and set her teeth on edge.
The rough texture of the fabric against his skin was enough to set his teeth on edge.

senses.definition: to cause someone to become extremely angry

examples.x: The blatant corruption and abuse of power by government officials made her blood boil with anger and frustration.
Hearing about the company's disregard for worker safety regulations made his blood boil, prompting him to take action and advocate for change.
When he discovered his car had been towed, he went ape and started yelling at the parking attendants.
The customer's rude behavior towards the staff made the manager go ape and demand they leave the premises immediately.
Seeing her ex-boyfriend with another woman at the party made her go ape and throw a drink in his face.
The referee's controversial decision caused the coach to go ape on the sidelines, shouting and gesturing in anger.
The constant delays and cancellations at the airport made the frustrated passengers go ape, venting their anger at the airline staff.

senses.definition: an imaginary pill that is recommended to an angry or anxious person to take as a way of asking them to relax or calm down

examples.x: Hey, take a chill pill and let's talk about this calmly.
His temper was flaring up, so I told him to take a chill pill and take a few deep breaths.
She could use a chill pill to calm her nerves before the presentation.
Whenever he starts getting worked up, I remind him to swallow a chill pill.
The situation calls for everyone to grab a chill pill and approach it with a level head.
The constant barking of the neighbor's dog was driving her up the wall.
The never-ending construction noise outside her apartment was driving her up the wall and interfering with her work.
The repetitive and monotonous tasks at his job were driving him up the wall, making him yearn for a change.
Her sibling's habit of playing loud music late at night was driving her up the wall and affecting her sleep.

senses.definition: used to tell or remind someone not to get too upset or excited about something

examples.x: Hey, keep your hair on!

word_list.pronunciation: hˈɪt stɹˈaɪk tˈʌtʃ ɐ ɹˈɔː sˈɛnsɪtˌɪv nˈɜːv

senses.definition: to mention or do something that causes someone to become upset or angry

examples.x: When she mentioned his ex-girlfriend, it struck a nerve and he became visibly upset.
The politician's controversial statement about taxes struck a nerve with the public.
His sarcastic comments about her appearance really struck a nerve, and she stormed out of the room.
Her criticism of his writing hit a nerve, and he became defensive.
When he mentioned her past mistakes, it hit a nerve and she felt a pang of guilt.
The teacher's comment about his lack of effort hit a nerve, motivating him to work harder.
Bringing up the topic of his failed business venture touched a nerve, and he became visibly uncomfortable.
The mention of her traumatic childhood experiences touched a nerve, and she started tearing up.
His insensitive comment about her weight touched a nerve, leaving her feeling self-conscious.
The criticism about her parenting skills hit a raw nerve, causing her to question her abilities.
Bringing up the topic of his unresolved family issues hit a raw nerve, and he abruptly changed the subject.
His comments about her insecurities struck a sensitive nerve, causing her to feel vulnerable.
The movie's portrayal of a tragic event struck a sensitive nerve, evoking strong emotions from the audience.
Discussing her weight in public touched a sensitive nerve, making her feel self-conscious and uncomfortable.

senses.definition: to intentionally make someone extremely angry

examples.x: The mischievous child loved to tease his sister and would do anything to get a rise out of her.
The prankster pulled a series of practical jokes on his friends to see if he could get a rise out of them and capture their reactions on camera.
